claudeA1b.claim: AppShellAside modal mode docblocks (app-shell.tsx:59 「Required:modal mode 走 Sheet → aria-labelledby 強制,per sheet.spec.md:98」 + L290 「title 強制(aria-labelledby per sheet.spec.md:98)」) assert that title prop wires aria-labelledby for the modal Sheet so screen readers can name the panel.
claudeA1b.codeReality:
CONFIRMED REAL. Modal mode (app-shell.tsx:324-335) renders containing `frame` (L304-321). `frame` renders

{title}

... — a bare

with NO id, NOT a . Verified: AppShell never imports SheetTitle/VisuallyHidden (grep returned only the two docblock comments), ChromeHeader injects no id/aria-labelledby (grep on chrome-header.tsx empty), and SheetContent at L327-332 receives only side/className/style — no aria-labelledby override. Radix react-dialog (node_modules/@radix-ui/react-dialog/dist/index.js) ALWAYS sets `aria-labelledby={context.titleId}` on Content (dist L278), but `id={context.titleId}` is attached to a real element ONLY when SheetTitle/DialogTitle renders (dist L299). With no SheetTitle, aria-labelledby points to an id no element carries → dangling reference (SR reads 'unnamed dialog') AND Radix TitleWarning effect (dist L338-350) runs `document.getElementById(titleId)` → not found → console.error('`Content` requires a `Title`...'). The docblock-claimed accessible-name wiring does not exist in the modal path. Note: desktop inline path (L342-353) correctly uses aria-label={title}, so defect is scoped to mobile/modal Sheet only.

claudeA1b.fileLine: app-shell.spec.md:189 (+ spec L66, anatomy stories L153)
claudeA1b.claim: Spec L189 「title prop required(per `sheet.spec.md:98` 禁無 title — `aria-labelledby` 強制)」 + spec L66 「modal mode 走 Sheet → `aria-labelledby` 強制」 + anatomy stories L153 「自動連結 `aria-labelledby`,讓螢幕報讀器讀得到面板名稱」 all claim the modal Aside auto-links aria-labelledby to a visible title.
claudeA1b.codeReality: CONFIRMED REAL (same root defect as the tsx finding, different documentation surface). sheet.spec.md 禁止事項 L98 explicitly prohibits: 「Sheet content 無 title — `aria-labelledby` 必須對應可見 title,SR 否則讀「unnamed dialog」」 — there is NO documented exception; the AppShell modal path actively violates its own cited canonical. Verified verbatim: spec.md L66/L189 and anatomy stories L153 all assert the auto-link, but app-shell.tsx:307 renders title as a bare <h2> (no id) inside ChromeHeader, never <SheetTitle>, so Radix's unconditional aria-labelledby has no target. The anatomy stories prose 「自動連結 aria-labelledby」 does not match real DOM. Fix: render <SheetTitle> (binds context.titleId) — wrap in VisuallyHidden if visual duplication unwanted — or pass explicit aria-labelledby to SheetContent + give the <h2> a matching id.

claudeA1b.claim: story note: readonly 仍然有 wrapper 高度和 padding...Table cell 用 `<Input mode='display'>`(純文字,無 wrapper)
claudeA1b.codeReality: input.tsx:123-156 confirmed: mode='display' renders a <div> wrapper with fieldWrapperStyles({mode:'display',variant:'default',size}) wrapping a <span>. The compoundVariant (field-wrapper.tsx:97-105) for mode:'display'+variant:'default' = 'bg-transparent border border-transparent !px-0 !py-0' — strips padding/bg/border (zero visual chrome) but does NOT strip height: base size variant (field-wrapper.tsx:61) 'md: ...h-field-md...' still applies. So display mode IS a wrapper <div> with the SAME h-field-md height as readonly — only padding differs. The note's parenthetical '無 wrapper'(no wrapper) is factually false (there IS a wrapper element), and the implied 'tighter/shorter than readonly' is wrong on the height axis (identical h-field-md); only horizontal px-3 padding is removed. Accurate description would be '無視覺 chrome / 無左右 padding' not '無 wrapper'.

claudeA1b.claim: props table mode 說明:'顯示模式，disabled/readOnly 原生屬性會自動覆蓋'
claudeA1b.codeReality: input.tsx:110-112 confirmed: resolvedMode = modeProp ?? fieldCtx?.mode ?? (readOnly ? 'readonly' : disabled ? 'disabled' : 'edit'). Native readOnly/disabled are the LOWEST-priority fallback in the ?? chain — they only resolve mode when no explicit mode prop AND no fieldCtx.mode. An explicit mode prop is NOT overridden by native disabled/readOnly (e.g. <Input mode='edit' readOnly> resolves to mode='edit', modeProp wins). The wording '原生屬性會自動覆蓋'(native attrs auto-override) describes the precedence backwards — it is a fallback (lowest), not an override (highest). Misleading doc text. Correct framing: 'mode 未顯式指定時，由 native readOnly/disabled fallback 推導'.
